revenue: $15.56 billion, an increase of 37.6% YoY compared to $11.30 billion in Q2 2024
net_income: $5.66 billion, an increase of 90.8% YoY compared to $2.97 billion in Q2 2024
key_metrics: Diluted EPS increased 91.8% YoY to $6.29 from $3.28
Research and development expenses increased 23.0% YoY to $3.34 billion
Inventory levels increased 45.1% to $11.01 billion from $7.59 billion at year-end 2024
qoq_changes: Revenue increased 22.2% sequentially to $15.56 billion in Q2 2025 from an implied $12.73 billion in Q1 2025
Net income more than doubled sequentially to $5.66 billion in Q2 2025 from an implied $2.76 billion in Q1 2025
new_risks: Risks associated with the use of artificial intelligence or other emerging technologies exacerbating competitive, regulatory, litigation, and cybersecurity vulnerabilities
Proliferation of counterfeit, misbranded, adulterated, or illegally compounded products
Supply chain unpredictability and variability in demand, including labor shortages and third-party performance issues
management_tone: Highly bullish, as evidenced by explosive top-line growth of 37.6% and a near-doubling of net income, reflecting massive market uptake and successful scaling of operations.
analyst_note: Eli Lilly delivered a blowout Q2 2025 with revenue up 37.6% and net income surging 90.8% YoY, driven by robust demand and expanding margins. The significant $3.4 billion inventory build since year-end suggests the company is aggressively scaling supply to meet unprecedented demand for its key therapies, though counterfeit products and supply chain variability remain key risks to monitor.
